text:
When Tomas Machac and Katerina Siniakova won an Olympic gold medal in mixed doubles at the 2024 Paris Games, they never would have thought their private lives would be thrust into the spotlight. Machac and Siniakova started their campaign to win gold for Czechia just weeks after a reported break-up.

But they still went all the way,beating Wang Xinyu and Zhang Zhizhen of Chinain the gold medal match - and then celebratingwith an emotional kiss on the podium. It set social media alight, and even became a talking point on daytime TV. But Machac and Siniakova kept their cards close to their chest, refusing to reveal whether or not they were still dating.

Reflecting on their unusual story more than a year later, Machac exclusively toldExpress Sport: “I think for the people around the story was very interesting and it’s not it’s like happening every day. So I guess it was huge for the world at that time.”

While the world was invested in Machac and Siniakova’s fairytale gold medal run, Machac himself barely had time to celebrate the victory before getting back on the tennis tour for the Masters 1000 in Canada and the final Grand Slam tournament of the season.

“That time for me, I didn't have too much time to think [about] what happened because I was going straight to Montreal and then the US Open. So when I started to realise, the shock was already gone, so I didn't live that time with this,” he explained.

Broken up or not, Machac and Siniakova maintained a great relationship during their run at the Olympics, and the ATP No. 32 still has nothing but praise for his mixed doubles partner. Siniakova is a doubles icon - she’s spent 174 weeks and counting as the WTA doubles No. 1 and has won 11 Grand Slam titles across women’s and mixed doubles.



“She’s, I will not say one of the best, I think she’s the best doubles player who ever played, so for me,” Machac said of the world No. 1. “I couldn't have had a better partner to play [with]. And for us, I think that two weeks was very interesting and the story was beautiful at that time. It’s good to play with her in the doubles for sure.”

Even though he partnered up with Siniakova, winning a gold medal in mixed doubles was still a surprise for singles specialist Machac, who now keeps his gold medal in a safe, and has no plans to get it out and wear it.

The 25-year-old continued: “For me, to win a doubles title was great, but for me, singles is the priority, of course. And it’s different, but the Olympics is something where you just want to win a medal in every sport. It doesn’t matter what you do. You just want to be the best. But it was interesting to have it in the mixed doubles because I wasn't that good. I wasn't good at all in mixed doubles, and that week I was playing great. [Siniakova] showed me how to do it!”

Machac was so determined to win a gold medal in Paris that he entered all three tennis events - singles, mixed doubles, and men’s doubles with Adam Pavlasek. As he advanced through the draw with Siniakova, the former world No. 20 managed to keep his composure right up until they found themselves at championship point.

“I think during the game you don't feel that much what can happen because there is no time. You just think about what you are doing every day, that’s tennis. So I remember it was 8-8 in the super tiebreak and I was feeling like it’s the beginning of the match,” he said.

“There was still lots of things to do, and I realised when there was a match point, I didn’t know how to hold the rackets for sure! So I was very, very nervous. It’s different [to winning a normal title] because you have an opportunity to win a title every week. The Olympic Games is once every four years. So you can be injured and you can miss the Olympics and you don’t have lots of chances.

“So I know that this thing is unique and you have to do everything possible. I remember, I was playing singles, doubles, mixed doubles, I was playing three competitions there. And every day since the beginning of the Olympics, I was going at the village, 1pm every day because there was lots of things to do. So [it was] very difficult.”

title: Olympic champion sent fans wild by kissing ex after winning gold medal | Tennis | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Yasmin Syed
description: EXCLUSIVE: Tomas Machac reflected on his memorable Olympic gold medal run after winning alongside his ex-girlfriend.
